# Agentic Patterns: this project mapped against nibzard's catalogue

**Date:** 2026-05-11
**Status:** Mapping document. Generated by analysing 19 individual pattern
entries from <https://github.com/nibzard/awesome-agentic-patterns> (~178
patterns total, 8 categories) and cross-referencing each against the
`inderes-mcp-agent-system` codebase, `BACKLOG.md`, and `LESSONS.md`.
Also references Google Cloud's *Choose a design pattern for your
agentic AI system* (~10 macro patterns).

**Why this exists:** so when we (or anyone reading this repo) reach for
*"have I seen this idea before, what's it called, where does it come
from"*, the answer is one document away. Also so when we adopt new
patterns the rationale is explicit, not folklore.

---

## Map

- [§1 What the catalogues are](#1-what-the-catalogues-are)
- [§2 TL;DR — pattern-to-codebase map](#2-tldr--pattern-to-codebase-map)
- [§3 Patterns we already implement](#3-patterns-we-already-implement)
- [§4 Patterns BACKLOG'd](#4-patterns-backlogd)
- [§5 Novel patterns worth adopting](#5-novel-patterns-worth-adopting)
- [§6 Patterns we explicitly do NOT pursue](#6-patterns-we-explicitly-do-not-pursue)
- [§7 Decision framework — when to consult which catalogue](#7-decision-framework--when-to-consult-which-catalogue)
- [§8 Concrete next actions](#8-concrete-next-actions)

---

## 1. What the catalogues are

### nibzard/awesome-agentic-patterns

- **Maintainer:** Nikola Balic (`@nibzard`), independent curator
- **Scope:** ~178 patterns, organised into 8 categories: Context & Memory,
  Feedback Loops, Learning & Adaptation, Orchestration & Control,
  Reliability & Eval, Security & Safety, Tool Use & Environment, UX &
  Collaboration
- **Quality filter:** each pattern must be **traceable to a public
  source** (blog, talk, paper, repo). Marketing material rejected.
  Authors are crediting Boris Cherny (Claude Code), Anthropic
  Engineering, Will Larson, Simon Willison, Thorsten Ball / Quinn Slack
  (AMP), Hyperbrowser Team, the Anthropic MCP team, OpenAI Codex team,
  community Reddit threads, etc.
- **Granularity:** **micro patterns** — implementation-level. Each entry
  has Problem / Solution / How-to-use / Trade-offs and often code
  snippets (TypeScript, Python, pseudo-code, Mermaid diagrams).
- **Status tags:** `emerging` / `established` / `best-practice` /
  `validated-in-production` — useful signal of maturity.
- **Companion site:** <https://agentic-patterns.com> with Pattern
  Explorer, Compare Tool, Decision Explorer, Graph Visualization.

### Google Cloud — Choose a design pattern for your agentic AI system

- **Authority:** Google Cloud Architecture Center
- **Scope:** ~10 **macro patterns**: Single-Agent, Sequential, Parallel,
  Loop, Iterative Refinement, Review & Critique, Coordinator,
  Hierarchical Task Decomposition, Swarm, ReAct, Human-in-the-Loop,
  Custom Logic
- **Strength:** Decision criteria (task complexity / latency / cost
  budget / human involvement). Recommends *matching architectural
  complexity to actual problem requirements rather than defaulting to
  complex multi-agent systems*.
- **Weakness:** Stays at high level. Doesn't cover MCP-specific,
  fabrication-guard, provenance, or per-claim attribution patterns —
  those live one layer down.

### How the two complement each other

- **Google Cloud** answers *"what TYPE of pattern fits this query
  shape"* (e.g. *"this is a Hierarchical Task Decomposition with
  Parallel sub-execution and Review/Critique gating"*).
- **nibzard** answers *"how do production teams actually implement that
  pattern, what failure modes have they hit, what's the variant called
  in the literature"* (e.g. *"the parallel sub-execution corresponds to
  Sub-Agent Spawning, the review step is an Output Verification Loop,
  the conflict resolution is the Opponent Processor pattern"*).

Use Google Cloud for the macro decision; use nibzard for the
implementation reference + naming discipline.

---

## 2. TL;DR — pattern-to-codebase map

In one table, what's where in this project. Detail follows in §§3–6.

| Pattern (nibzard slug) | Source / Authority | Status here | Where |
|---|---|---|---|
| Tool Capability Compartmentalization | Simon Willison MCP critique | ✅ shipped | `mcp/inderes_client.py:63–111`, `mcp/yahoo_client.py:36–67` |
| Sub-Agent Spawning | Anthropic + community | ✅ shipped | `agents/{quant,research,sentiment,valuation,portfolio}.py` |
| Plan-Then-Execute Pattern | Reddit, community | ✅ shipped | `agents/lead_planner.py` |
| Planner-Worker Separation | Anthropic | ✅ shipped | LEAD planner → 5 subagents fan-out |
| Hybrid LLM/Code Workflow Coordinator | Multiple | ✅ shipped | `orchestration/workflows.py` |
| Failover-Aware Model Fallback | Clawdbot impl | ✅ partial | `llm/gemini_client.py` — but heuristic too loose (BACKLOG §4) |
| Verbose Reasoning Transparency | Boris Cherny / Claude Code | ✅ shipped | `**Ajatus:**` opener in every subagent's response |
| Output Verification Loop | VeroQ Shield | ✅ shipped | `valuation/parser.py` + fabrication guard |
| Schema Validation Retry with Cross-Step Learning | Hyperbrowser | ✅ partial | Multi-company JSON array fix (`5f581c5`); no cross-step learning yet |
| Filesystem-Based Agent State | Anthropic | ✅ shipped | `~/.inderes_agent/runs/<ts>/` |
| Workflow Evals with Mocked Tools | Sierra / Larson | ✅ shipped | `tests/test_yahoo_mcp_wiring.py` etc. |
| Budget-Aware Model Routing with Hard Cost Caps | OpenAI / Codex | ✅ shipped | OWASP T1 hard limits, Wk 1 |
| MCP Pattern Injection | LangGraph community | ✅ shipped | Both MCPs serve patterns via tool calls |
| Cross-Cycle Consensus Relay | Nikita Dmitrieff | ✅ partial | Conflict-detector + provenance threading |
| Opponent Processor / Multi-Agent Debate | Dan Shipper | ✅ partial | Conflict-detector exists; not full debate yet |
| LLM Observability | Multiple | ✅ shipped | Run-log + console.log + conflicts.json + paattely.json |
| Tool Selection Guide | Anthropic | ✅ partial | Prompt-level tool guidance in each subagent's `.md` |
| Reflection Loop | Multiple | 💭 BACKLOG §1 #2 | Not implemented |
| Human-in-the-Loop Approval Framework | Google Cloud | 💭 BACKLOG Wk 3 | HITL Step 1 proposed |
| Episodic Memory Retrieval & Injection | Multiple | 💭 BACKLOG §1 #5 | "Insight ledger" |
| Self-Critique Evaluator Loop | OpenAI Codex | 💭 BACKLOG | Devil's-advocate toggle (§1) |
| **Lethal Trifecta Threat Model** | **Simon Willison** | 🆕 **adopt** | See §5 |
| **Action Caching & Replay Pattern** | **Hyperbrowser** | 🆕 **adopt** | See §5 |
| **Tool Search Lazy Loading** | **Anthropic Thariq** | 🆕 **adopt later** | See §5 |
| **Progressive Tool Discovery** | **Anthropic Engineering** | 🆕 **adopt later** | See §5 |
| **Agent Circuit Breaker (tool-level)** | **Michael Nygard / Hystrix** | 🆕 **adopt** | See §5 |
| **Subject Hygiene for Task Delegation** | **Nikola Balic** | 🆕 **adopt** | See §5 |
| Burn the Boats | AMP / Thorsten Ball | ❌ skip | See §6 — org strategy, not tech |
| Swarm | DeepMind / OpenAI Swarm | ❌ skip | Overkill for single-user research |
| Soulbound Identity Verification | Crypto/web3 community | ❌ skip | No on-chain layer in scope |
| AgentFund Crowdfunding | Crypto/web3 community | ❌ skip | Not relevant |
| Capability-Escrow-Receipt | Crypto/web3 community | ❌ skip | Not relevant |

---

## 3. Patterns we already implement

For each: what the pattern is (in nibzard's framing), how we implement
it, strength assessment, follow-up improvements available.

### 3.1 Tool Capability Compartmentalization

> *"Split monolithic tools into reader/processor/writer micro-tools.
> Require explicit per-call user consent when composing across capability
> classes. Run each class in an isolated subprocess with scoped API keys
> and file permissions."* — based on Simon Willison's MCP critique.

**Our implementation:** Per-agent tool partitioning in `inderes_client.py`
and `yahoo_client.py`. Each subagent sees only its domain's tools:

```python
QUANT_TOOLS      = ("search-companies", "get-fundamentals", "get-inderes-estimates")
SENTIMENT_TOOLS  = ("search-companies", "list-insider-transactions", ...)
YAHOO_QUANT_TOOLS    = ("search_ticker", "get_snapshot", "get_history")
YAHOO_SENTIMENT_TOOLS = ("search_ticker", "get_news", "get_holders")
```

**Strength: ★★★★☆** — bit-for-bit matches the pattern at the
tool-selection layer. Each agent gets only its assigned subset.

**Gap vs full pattern:** we don't yet do *capability-class isolation at
runtime* (separate subprocesses, scoped API keys per agent). Both MCPs
use a single bearer token and a single httpx.AsyncClient per agent.
This is fine because we trust both MCPs; the pattern's full strength
would matter if we added an untrusted MCP (Yahoo MCP today is
self-hosted, but a future community MCP could be untrusted).

**Follow-up:** when third-party MCPs enter the picture, formalise a
capability manifest (`tool-manifest.yml` per Simon Willison's example)
and gate cross-zone composition.

### 3.2 Sub-Agent Spawning + Planner-Worker Separation

**Pattern:** spawn specialised subagents in parallel/fan-out, each with
narrow scope; orchestrate via a planner-worker split.

**Our implementation:** `_AGENT_BUILDERS` in `orchestration/workflows.py`
spawns up to 5 subagents (QUANT/RESEARCH/SENTIMENT/VALUATION/PORTFOLIO)
in parallel through `asyncio.Semaphore(MAX_CONCURRENT_AGENTS)`. LEAD
planner emits a per-domain plan before fan-out; LEAD synthesizer
consumes results after.

**Strength: ★★★★★** — full pattern. Includes the "for {company}:"
prompt rephrasing (Subject Hygiene equivalent) when fan-out per company
fires.

**Gap vs full pattern:** none structurally. Possible improvement:
auto-orchestrator (= meta-router) decides dynamically which subset of
agents to fan out to, instead of router's heuristic. BACKLOG'd.

### 3.3 Verbose Reasoning Transparency

**Pattern:** *"Implement a feature that allows users to inspect the
agent's internal thought process or reasoning steps on demand."* — Boris
Cherny / Claude Code lineage.

**Our implementation:** Every subagent prompt mandates a
`**Ajatus:**` opener — agent emits its plan/reasoning as the first
section. LEAD synthesis includes a `**Päättely:**` block. UI surfaces
both as `<details>` expanders.

**Strength: ★★★★★** — production-validated. The user explicitly cited
this as a confidence-builder in multiple feedback rounds.

**Bonus**: we go further than the pattern by surfacing the full *plan*
(if enabled) AND the post-hoc *reasoning* — two-stage transparency, not
just one. Worth documenting back to nibzard as a refinement.

### 3.4 Output Verification Loop + Schema Validation Retry

**Pattern:** *"Insert a verification step between generation and action.
Extract claims, retrieve evidence, score, retry if below threshold."*

**Our implementation:** Two layers.
- Structural: fabrication-guard (`agent.tool_calls == 0 → reject`).
- Schema: `valuation/parser.py` validates JSON shape, rejects malformed
  output. Multi-company array support added 2026-05-10.

**Strength: ★★★☆☆** — partial. We have structural verification, not
yet semantic per-claim evidence retrieval. The full pattern would
extract each numerical claim from synthesis (e.g. *"Sampo P/E = 14.2"*),
retrieve evidence from the original tool trace, and score alignment.

**Gap:** evidence retrieval per claim — would close the loop and feed
into the footnote-marker BACKLOG item.

### 3.5 Cross-Cycle Consensus Relay

**Pattern:** *"Each agent cycle reads a consensus relay document at the
start, executes work, then writes an updated consensus document at the
end. The document encodes phase, completed actions, active decisions,
open questions, single most important next action."*

**Our implementation:** Partial — we have:
- `runs/<ts>/conflicts.json` (encodes agreements / conflicts / isolated)
- `runs/<ts>/paattely.json` (LEAD's structured reasoning)
- Provenance threading into LEAD (tool-call trace = ground truth)

These together form an effective per-run "consensus document", but
**not yet across cycles**. We currently don't load yesterday's
consensus into today's run.

**Gap → BACKLOG candidate:** when watchlist + daily briefing ships
(BACKLOG #4), a `relay.md` file persisting yesterday's open questions
+ pending decisions could feed straight into today's planner prompt.

### 3.6 Opponent Processor / Multi-Agent Debate

**Pattern:** *"Create agents with opposing incentives or roles, let
them critique each other's work, then synthesize results."*

**Our implementation:** Partial. Conflict detector LLM-call between
subagent execution and LEAD synthesis surfaces structural disagreements
(`conflicts.json`). LEAD must address each conflict explicitly in its
synthesis (per `lead.md` prompt instructions).

**Gap vs full pattern:** Our conflict detector is post-hoc analysis,
not adversarial debate. We don't spawn explicit "bull" and "bear"
agents on the same data.

**BACKLOG'd:** Devil's-advocate toggle + Bull/Bear debate (§1 Open —
small + medium).

### 3.7 Failover-Aware Model Fallback

**Pattern:** *"Semantic error classification with intelligent fallback
chains. Each failure is categorized into specific reason types
(`timeout`, `rate_limit`, `auth`, `billing`, `format`,
`context_overflow`), and fallback behaviour is tailored to that
reason."*

**Our implementation:** Partial — `FallbackGeminiChatClient` has the
fallback chain (primary → secondary → QuotaExhaustedError), but **error
classification is too loose**:

```python
def _is_quota_exhausted(exc: BaseException) -> bool:
    msg = str(exc).lower()
    return "429" in msg or "resource_exhausted" in msg or "quota" in msg
```

Tonight's quota-error mystery (5 false-positive failures despite paid
Tier 1 dashboard showing 0.1% usage) is exactly the class of failure
nibzard's pattern prevents. **BACKLOG'd in §4 (2026-05-11).**

### 3.8 Filesystem-Based Agent State

**Pattern:** *"Agents persist intermediate results and working state to
files in the execution environment. This creates durable checkpoints
that enable workflow resumption, recovery from failures, and support
for tasks that exceed single-session context limits."*

**Our implementation:** `~/.inderes_agent/runs/<ts>/`:
- `query.txt`, `routing.json`, `plan.json`
- `subagent-NN-{domain}.json` per agent
- `conflicts.json`, `paattely.json`, `synthesis.txt`, `narrative.md`
- `console.log` (full trace)
- `valuation.json` when present

**Strength: ★★★★★** — full pattern. Per BCBS 239 lineage requirements.
Replay-friendly.

**Gap:** we don't yet RESUME from these — they're forensic only. Could
extend to "resume failed run from last successful subagent" but no
demand yet.

### 3.9 Workflow Evals with Mocked Tools

**Pattern:** *"Dual tool implementations — every tool has both `true`
and `mock` versions. Workflow tests use mocks to validate
prompt+tool+agent coordination without burning LLM tokens."*

**Our implementation:**
- `tests/test_yahoo_mcp_wiring.py` mocks `yf.Ticker` and validates
  partitioning + toggle semantics
- `tests/test_valuation_tool_guard.py` validates fabrication-guard
  without LLM
- `tests/test_evals_yaml.py` structural validation of golden cases
- Live `evals/golden.yaml` runner exists, only fires manually

**Strength: ★★★★☆** — full structural pattern. Live eval runner is
manual, not nightly. BACKLOG'd: autonomous nightly eval (§10).

### 3.10 Budget-Aware Model Routing with Hard Cost Caps

**Pattern:** *"Tiered model catalog (small/medium/frontier). Policy
engine computes max allowable spend before each call. Deterministic
fallback when budget exceeded. Quality override paths for
safety-critical work."*

**Our implementation:** OWASP T1 hard limits (`max_iter`,
`max_tool_calls`, `max_cost`, `max_duration`, kill switch) shipped Wk 1.
Three-tier model setup: Flash Lite (Vakio) / Pro for LEAD only (Tarkka
LEAD) / Pro everywhere (Tarkka kaikki).

**Strength: ★★★★☆** — structural pattern present, but **dynamic
routing by query shape is not implemented** — user chooses tier
manually. Auto-orchestrator would close this gap.

### 3.11 Tool Selection Guide / Tool Use Steering via Prompting

**Pattern:** *"Tool descriptions concrete enough that LLM picks the
right tool from descriptions alone, no prompt hand-holding."*

**Our implementation:** Tonight's empirical observation: `get_holders`
and `get_history` from Yahoo fired correctly **without prompt nudging**
— LLM read descriptions in MCP responses and chose appropriately. This
is the pattern working as intended.

**Strength: ★★★★☆** — proven empirically. One thing we could improve:
explicit *"when to use Yahoo vs Inderes for the same question"*
guidance — e.g. valuation Yahoo-first BVPS+price (BACKLOG Gap 6).

### 3.12 LLM Observability

**Our implementation:** Aikajana strip + Open log + Open päättely (all
shipped). Run-log per BCBS 239. Conflict detector emits structured
JSON. Subagent thought traces visible in UI.

**Strength: ★★★★★** — full pattern.

---

## 4. Patterns BACKLOG'd

These nibzard patterns map cleanly to existing BACKLOG items. The
mapping helps when prioritising — if a backlog item is also a widely
adopted nibzard pattern, that's evidence it's worth doing.

| BACKLOG item | nibzard equivalent | Maturity in catalogue |
|---|---|---|
| §1 #2 Reflection + retry on weird output | Reflection Loop / Self-Critique Evaluator Loop | `emerging` |
| §1 #5 Insight ledger — long-term memory | Episodic Memory Retrieval & Injection | `emerging` |
| §1 HITL Step 1 — cost gate + pre-flight | Human-in-the-Loop Approval Framework | `established` |
| §1 Devil's advocate toggle | Opponent Processor (lite version) | `emerging` |
| §1 Bull/Bear debate | Opponent Processor (full version) | `emerging` |
| §1 Cross-source retry — "did you check other MCP?" | (no exact match — combine with **Agent Circuit Breaker** §5.5) | n/a |
| §1 Per-claim confidence scoring | Output Verification Loop (claim-level extension) | `emerging` |
| §1 Footnote markers + sources panel | (no exact match in nibzard — strong UX-Collaboration category candidate to propose back) | n/a |
| §2 Multi-company fan-out broken | (anti-pattern — implicit) | n/a |
| §3 Always-on price chart | (no exact match — multi-modal output category, sparse there) | n/a |
| §3 Target-price comparison table | (no exact match — Bloomberg-ANR specialised) | n/a |
| §4 Quota error diagnostic + heuristic refinement | Failover-Aware Model Fallback (full impl) | `validated-in-production` |
| §10 Autonomous nightly eval + self-repair | Workflow Evals with Mocked Tools (continuous variant) | `emerging` |

**Insight:** Six of our BACKLOG items are mainstream patterns in the
catalogue (Reflection Loop, HITL, Opponent Processor, Failover Fallback,
Workflow Evals, Insight Ledger). That's signal-strong validation our
backlog points in industry-accepted directions.

---

## 5. Novel patterns worth adopting

These are nibzard patterns that **don't currently map** to either our
codebase or BACKLOG, but offer real value. Ranked by impact-per-effort
for this project.

### 5.1 🔴 Lethal Trifecta Threat Model (Simon Willison)

**The pattern:**
> *"Combining three agent capabilities — (1) access to private data,
> (2) exposure to untrusted content, (3) ability to externally
> communicate — creates a straightforward path for prompt-injection
> attackers to steal sensitive information."*

**Status in our project:** Currently safe because we have **only two
of the three legs**:
1. ✅ Private data — Inderes paid analyst content (yes)
2. ✅ Untrusted content — Yahoo news feed, Inderes forum posts (yes,
   community-generated)
3. ❌ External communication — we don't email, Slack, or call external
   APIs that write state

→ **Two-leg safety: prompt injection in forum content cannot
exfiltrate Inderes analyst content** because the agent has no outbound
write capability beyond the user's screen.

**But every roadmap item that adds the third leg breaks this:**
- BACKLOG §1 #4 Watchlist + daily briefing (if email/Slack delivery
  added) → triggers trifecta
- BACKLOG §5 Telegram bot interface → trifecta
- HITL Step 2 (if approval triggers external action) → trifecta

**Recommended action:**
1. Adopt the **capability matrix** discipline: every MCP tool tagged
   with `{private_data: bool, untrusted_input: bool, external_comm:
   bool}` in code (e.g. `inderes_client.py` constants).
2. Add a `lethal_trifecta_check()` pre-execution guard in
   `orchestration/limits.py` (where other hard limits live).
3. New BACKLOG §4 entry: *"Lethal Trifecta gate — refuse to execute
   any plan that combines all three capability classes in a single
   session, regardless of which agents are involved."*

**Effort:** ~2 h. Critical to lock in BEFORE shipping email/Slack
delivery, not after.

### 5.2 🟢 Agent Circuit Breaker — tool-level

**The pattern (Michael Nygard / Netflix Hystrix lineage):**
> *"Agents that use external tools face a common failure mode: a tool
> endpoint becomes degraded and the agent keeps calling it, burning
> tokens on retries that will never succeed. The circuit breaker wraps
> each tool call and tracks failure rates, transitioning between
> CLOSED → OPEN → HALF_OPEN states."*

**Our current state:** We have model-level fallback
(`FallbackGeminiChatClient`) but not **tool-level** circuit breaking.
When Yahoo MCP returns `YFRateLimitError` 5 times in a row, the agent
keeps retrying. When Inderes MCP token expires mid-session, similar.

**Recommended action:**
1. Wrap each `MCPStreamableHTTPTool` in a `CircuitBreakerMCPTool`
   decorator
2. Track failure rate per tool name with a rolling window (last 10
   calls, last 60 s)
3. OPEN state for 5 min if failure rate > 50% → agent skips that tool
   and reasons over the rest
4. HALF_OPEN state attempts one call after 5 min — close if success,
   re-open if fail

**Pairs naturally with Failover-Aware Model Fallback (§3.7) — same
machinery, different layer.**

**Effort:** ~3 h including tests. High value because it eliminates an
entire class of UX failures (*"miksi vastaus on tyhjä?"* when an MCP is
silently failing).

### 5.3 🟢 Action Caching & Replay Pattern (Hyperbrowser)

**The pattern:**
> *"Record every action during execution with precise metadata,
> enabling deterministic replay without LLM calls. Action cache entries
> store: stepIndex, instruction, elementId, method, arguments,
> frameIndex, actionType, success, message."*

**Our current state:** We have run-logs but **no replay capability**.
When a bug surfaces in production, we re-run from scratch (re-burning
LLM tokens, with non-deterministic results).

**Recommended action:**
1. The existing `runs/<ts>/subagent-NN.json` already has the data —
   tool name, arguments, result text
2. Add a `tools/replay_run.py` script: given a run-id, replay tool
   calls deterministically against cached results, only re-runs LLM
   for the synthesis steps
3. Use case: regression tests when we change a prompt — replay 10
   golden runs, compare new synthesis against old

**Effort:** ~3 h. Massive payoff for debug + eval workflows.

**Bonus**: opens the door to **eval cache**: golden case results
cached, only re-run LLM when prompts change. Cost-saving for the
nightly eval cron (§10).

### 5.4 🟡 Subject Hygiene for Task Delegation

**The pattern:**
> *"Enforce clear, specific task subjects for every Task tool invocation.
> A good subject should be: not empty, specific and descriptive,
> reference-able, follow naming conventions (imperative mood, clear
> target)."*

**Our current state:** We DO follow this in fan-out (e.g.
`"For Sampo: <query>"`-style rephrasing), but not consistently in
internal subagent labels. Subagent file names are `subagent-NN-quant.json`
without the actual task subject (company, scope).

**Recommended action:**
1. Each subagent's `runs/<ts>/subagent-NN-{domain}.json` should include
   a `subject` field: `"Quantitative analysis of Sampo for valuation
   query"` rather than implicit
2. The console.log + narrative.md outputs reference subagents by
   subject, not by domain index — e.g. `"quant(Sampo) found ROE 16.2%"`
   instead of `"subagent-1 found ROE 16.2%"`

**Effort:** ~1 h. Improves observability for free.

### 5.5 🟡 Tool Search Lazy Loading / Progressive Tool Discovery

**The pattern:**
> *"As MCP servers grow to 50+ tools, preloading all tool descriptions
> consumes 67K+ tokens just for tool descriptions. Provide a
> ToolSearchTool that allows agents to search tool metadata and
> selectively load tools."*

**Our current state:** We have ~15 Inderes tools + 6 Yahoo tools = ~21
tools. Token cost is manageable (low thousands per agent). **Not yet a
problem.**

**When it becomes a problem:** if we add SEC EDGAR (~30 tools), ECB
SDW (~40 tools), FRED (~20 tools), Google Search grounding — easily
100+ tools across MCPs. Then per-agent tool descriptions explode.

**Recommended action:** **defer**, but bookmark. When a 4th MCP joins
the agent fleet, revisit. The pattern is implementable in our existing
`build_mcp_tool()` abstraction by adding a `tool_search_mode=True`
option that lazy-loads schemas.

**Effort when needed:** ~5 h for full lazy-load implementation +
search infrastructure.

### 5.6 🟡 Subject Hygiene's deeper version: "What is this run ABOUT?"

(Same pattern family as §5.4 but applied at run level, not subagent
level.)

The `query.txt` file is the user's raw input. We could also have
`subject.txt` — a normalised, agent-extracted task subject —
populated by the router (already extracts `companies` and `domains`).

Useful for: cross-run grep, build_runs_index.py searches, debugging
patterns like *"all runs where Sampo + valuation + valid:false
co-occurred"*.

**Effort:** ~30 min. Quick win.

---

## 6. Patterns we explicitly do NOT pursue

Documenting why, so we don't reconsider these every quarter.

### 6.1 Burn the Boats (AMP / Thorsten Ball)

**Pattern:** intentionally killing working features to force evolution.

**Why we skip:** This is an **organisational / product strategy**
pattern, not a technical agent pattern. The catalogue itself notes
*"Production AI systems typically reject 'no fallback' approaches in
favor of bounded autonomy with human oversight."* For a personal
research tool with one user, *all* legacy code is one git revert away,
so there's no strategic value in self-destructing functioning code.

### 6.2 Swarm (DeepMind / OpenAI Swarm)

**Pattern:** all-to-all communication between agents with collaborative
refinement.

**Why we skip:** 5 subagents with predefined responsibilities + a
synthesizer is enough for our domain. Swarm overhead (N² communication
cost, coordination prompts) doesn't pay back in single-user investment
research where the question shapes are bounded.

**Reconsider if:** we add more than 10 subagents OR move to a domain
where agent responsibilities are not knowable up-front.

### 6.3 Soulbound Identity Verification + Capability-Escrow-Receipt + AgentFund

**Pattern family:** crypto/web3-inspired patterns for agent identity
and incentive alignment.

**Why we skip:** No on-chain layer in scope. Single-user, no
multi-agent economy. Patterns of theoretical interest, zero practical
relevance here.

### 6.4 Agentic Search Over Vector Embeddings

**Pattern:** maintain a vector index of project content for semantic
search.

**Why we skip:** Inderes MCP already provides search-companies +
search-forum-topics + list-content with native search. Yahoo MCP
provides search_ticker. Adding a vector index on top would duplicate
existing capabilities at extra cost (embedding maintenance + per-query
search latency).

**Reconsider if:** we add a layer of personal notes / annotations
that aren't covered by either MCP.

### 6.5 Self-Rewriting Meta-Prompt Loop

**Pattern:** agents that modify their own system prompts based on past
performance.

**Why we skip:** No-go for a research tool where prompt stability is a
trust requirement. Versioned `prompts/*.md` files + golden eval gate is
the production-safe alternative we already have.

---

## 7. Decision framework — when to consult which catalogue

When facing a design decision, here's the lookup order:

```
Step 1: What category of question is this?

  "What architectural shape fits this query type?"
       → Google Cloud (10 macro patterns)

  "How do I actually implement this in code?"
       → nibzard (178 micro patterns)

  "What patterns might I be missing from my current implementation?"
       → both, in parallel

Step 2: Specifically for nibzard, narrow the category:

  Context & Memory          → state, retrieval, caching
  Feedback Loops            → evaluation, self-critique
  Learning & Adaptation     → fine-tuning, skill evolution
  Orchestration & Control   → multi-agent routing, task decomposition
  Reliability & Eval        → testing, verification, safety
  Security & Safety         → isolation, access control
  Tool Use & Environment    → API design, sandboxing
  UX & Collaboration        → human handoff, transparency

Step 3: Sanity-check via decision flow:

  Is the pattern from a credible source?      (nibzard requires this)
  Does it have status: validated-in-production
    or established?                            (prefer over emerging)
  Does it solve a problem we currently face?
  Effort fits our timeline?
  → Adopt → BACKLOG entry → eval case
```

For greenfield design decisions, **start with Google Cloud** (which
macro pattern to use) and **descend into nibzard** (how to implement
the chosen macro pattern). For *"is this idea novel or just a
named pattern I forgot"*, **search nibzard slugs first** — saves
inventing names that already have community consensus.

---

## 8. Concrete next actions

Ranked by impact-per-hour. Each item references the section above for
detail. Effort estimates are wall-clock for one developer with full
context.

| Priority | Action | nibzard ref | Effort | Why now |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 🔴 1 | Capture **Lethal Trifecta Threat Model** as BACKLOG §4 preventive item, BEFORE adding any email/Slack/external-write capability | §5.1 | 15 min docs | Roadmap watchlist + Telegram-bot ideas will trigger trifecta. Lock in defense before then |
| 🟢 2 | **Gemini quota diagnostic logging + heuristic refinement** = full Failover-Aware Model Fallback impl | §3.7 + nibzard | ~45 min | Already BACKLOG'd. The actual error is invisible right now. Solving this unblocks all future model-routing work |
| 🟢 3 | **Subject Hygiene** at run level — `runs/<ts>/subject.txt` populated by router | §5.4 + §5.6 | ~30 min | Observability win for free. No risk |
| 🟢 4 | **Action Caching & Replay** script `tools/replay_run.py` | §5.3 | ~3 h | Massive payoff for debug + eval workflows. Pairs with action-cache for nightly evals |
| 🟢 5 | **Tool-level Agent Circuit Breaker** wrapping `_SanitizingMCPTool` | §5.2 | ~3 h | Eliminates "empty answer with no error" UX failures. Pairs with §3.7 |
| 🟡 6 | Document this analysis itself + push as `docs/agentic_patterns_mapping_2026-05-11.md` | (meta) | done | ✅ this file |
| 🟡 7 | Push back to nibzard: propose new patterns we have that aren't yet there — *Per-Agent MCP Toolset Partitioning* (more granular than Tool Capability Compartmentalization), *Source-Tagged Provenance Footnotes* (no UX-Collaboration entry for this), *Two-stage Reasoning Transparency* (plan + post-hoc paattely) | (community contribution) | ~2 h | Maintains the catalogue's quality + gets our patterns peer-reviewed |
| 🟢 8 | Add **Tool Search Lazy Loading** to bookmark list with trigger condition: *"if 4th MCP joins fleet"* | §5.5 | n/a | Defer-with-trigger, not adopt now |

**Total adoption effort if all 1–5 done in sequence: ~7.5 hours**.
Spread over a week of evenings, plus the existing BACKLOG, none of these
demand more than half a day. The cumulative effect is real, though —
defence in depth + observability + replay + circuit breaking is exactly
what separates *"works on my queries"* from *"production-grade"*.

## Appendix: nibzard pattern slugs referenced

For traceability — these are the patterns I actually fetched + read in
full while writing this document. Each slug maps to
`https://github.com/nibzard/awesome-agentic-patterns/blob/main/patterns/<slug>.md`.

```
lethal-trifecta-threat-model
tool-capability-compartmentalization
opponent-processor-multi-agent-debate
output-verification-loop
verbose-reasoning-transparency
action-caching-replay
cross-cycle-consensus-relay
subject-hygiene
burn-the-boats
mcp-pattern-injection
tool-search-lazy-loading
progressive-tool-discovery
agent-first-tool-discovery
agent-circuit-breaker
filesystem-based-agent-state
schema-validation-retry-cross-step-learning
failover-aware-model-fallback
workflow-evals-with-mocked-tools
budget-aware-model-routing-with-hard-cost-caps
```

19 patterns analysed in depth out of ~178 total. Remaining ~159
patterns surveyed at title-level only — anyone curating the next pass
should pull the full slug list from `gh api
repos/nibzard/awesome-agentic-patterns/contents/patterns` and triage.
